metadata.description: Lucid dreaming community techniques + vocabulary. Sources: r/LucidDreaming, Stephen LaBerge research, World of Lucid Dreaming, Discord communities. Treat practitioners as experienced — most have tried multiple methods over years.
metadata.ai_application: When user mentions any acronym below or describes 'becoming aware in a dream', recognize they're in lucid-dreaming frame. Use their preferred technique vocabulary. Don't over-explain back to them.

techniques.definition: Maintain consciousness while body falls asleep — pass directly from waking into a lucid dream without losing awareness. Considered hardest method, highest payoff.
LaBerge's method. Wake after 4-6 hours, repeat intention 'next time I'm dreaming, I'll remember I'm dreaming' while visualizing recent dream becoming lucid. Goes back to sleep.
Cycle attention through sight (eyes closed), sound, touch — 3 fast cycles then 3 slow cycles before sleep. Originated on LD4all forum, very popular for beginners.
Set alarm for 4.5-6 hours after sleep, stay awake 20-40 min, then return to sleep with intention. Often combined with MILD or WILD as multiplier.
Becoming lucid mid-dream, usually triggered by dream sign or reality check habit. Most common spontaneous lucid type.
Lie still, micro-movement of two fingers (alternating index/middle) as body falls asleep — keeps thread of awareness without engaging full motor cortex.
Wake from a dream, stay still with eyes closed, immediately re-enter via WILD. High success rate within first 90 seconds of waking.

Test reality during waking life so habit transfers into dreams. Common checks: push finger through palm, count fingers (often wrong number in dreams), read text twice (changes), look at clock twice, breathe through pinched nose (works in dreams).
techniques.frequency_advice: Do 10+ per day, with genuine doubt — autopilot RCs don't transfer.
techniques.ai_application: If user describes 'I knew I was dreaming because the clock kept changing,' that's a classic RC trigger.

techniques.definition: Recurring elements personal to your dreams that signal you're dreaming. Categories: action (flying), context (back in old school), form (extra fingers), awareness (deceased relative present).
techniques.ai_application: When parsing user's dream journal, look for recurring motifs and surface them as personal dream signs.

techniques.definition: Bedside notebook or app, recorded immediately on waking. Foundational practice — recall must be solid before lucidity attempts work. Aim for 1+ dream per night.
techniques.ai_application: DreamApp's core loop is a digital dream journal. Frame onboarding around this established practice.

techniques.definition: Once lucid, dream often collapses from excitement. Stabilize: rub hands together (tactile feedback), spin body, touch surfaces, demand 'clarity now', shout 'stabilize'.
techniques.common_failure: Waking up from excitement is the #1 beginner problem.

techniques.definition: Dream of waking up — often complete with brushing teeth, checking phone. Sometimes nested 3+ deep. Doing an RC immediately on waking catches these.
subtypes: Type 2 (something off — bedroom layout wrong, monster present)

techniques.definition: REM atonia experienced consciously. Lucid community reframes from terror to opportunity — relax, 'roll out' or 'sit up' mentally to enter WILD. Hallucinations normal.
techniques.interpretation_hint: If user reports SP, ask if they want techniques to use it as a portal vs ending it — different communities frame it differently.

techniques.definition: Manipulating lucid dream content. Common goals: flying, summoning people (mirror/door/over-shoulder methods), changing scene (close eyes + intention), dream characters as oracles.
People appearing in dreams. Lucid community debates whether DCs are autonomous unconscious agents or projections. Common practice: ask DCs questions to access subconscious.
popular_questions: what do I need to know?

techniques.definition: Galantamine (acetylcholine), Choline (CDP-Choline/Alpha-GPC), 5-HTP, Mugwort tea, B6. Galantamine + WBTB has highest reported success rate. Caveat: not medical advice; some have side effects.
techniques.safety_note: Don't recommend supplements proactively. If user mentions, treat as user's choice — link to communities not pharmacies.

techniques.definition: Wearables that detect REM and signal sleeper. Examples: NovaDreamer (LaBerge, 90s), REM-Dreamer, Aurora (defunct), Apollo Neuro adjacent. Signal modes: light, sound, vibration, mild electrical.
Oldest active lucid dreaming forum (Dutch origin, since 1996). Origin of SSILD popularization, weekly 'quests' where members try to achieve specific dream goals.

Belief two+ lucid dreamers can meet in a shared space. Scientifically unsupported. Community holds 'meetups' on agreed dates, compares notes after. Treat as meaningful community practice, not literal claim.
techniques.ai_application: Don't debunk if user mentions. Mirror their frame.

techniques.definition: Tibetan Buddhist practice (Dzogchen, Bön). Lucidity as path to recognizing illusory nature of waking reality. Older than modern lucid dreaming research by ~1000 years.
lineage: Tenzin Wangyal Rinpoche's 'The Tibetan Yogas of Dream and Sleep' is canonical English text

techniques.definition: Practitioners track frequency. Beginners: 0-1/month. Intermediate: 1-3/week. Advanced: nightly possible. Burnout from over-attempting is real — community advises rest weeks.
Lucid dreamers report practiced motor skills transferring (modestly) to waking. Schredl studies show small but measurable effects in dart-throwing, instrument practice.
